pi_items.source: This is a destination path specified by the task, not a property of the data itself. Unverifiable without running the task.

epitools R package documentation (https://rdrr.io/cran/epitools/man/oswego.html) and epiDisplay package documentation (https://rdrr.io/cran/epiDisplay/man/Oswego.html). Column names match the expected fields in the actual Oswego dataset.

Epi Info 7 User Guide - Com Ref Select command (https://www.cdc.gov/epiinfo/user-guide/command-reference/analysis-commands-select.html) and Yes-No Field documentation (https://www.cdc.gov/epiinfo/user-guide/form-designer/how-to-use-fields-yes-no.html). In Epi Info, Yes-No fields use (+) for yes and (-) for no in SELECT statements, not 'Yes'/'No' string literals.

CDC and epidemiological sources (https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/epitools/versions/0.5-10.1/topics/oswego). The Oswego outbreak had 75 persons interviewed, of which 46 reported gastrointestinal illness. The min_rows value of 40 is conservative but correct since 46 > 40.

CDC and epidemiological sources (https://www.rdocumentation.org/packages/epitools/versions/0.5-10.1/topics/oswego). The Oswego outbreak had 46 ill cases, which is within the max_rows boundary of 50 (46 < 50).

CDC EIS publication (https://www.cdc.gov/eis/downloads/xoswego-401-303-student.pdf) and multiple epidemiological sources documenting the Oswego outbreak. Dr. A. M. Rubin completed interviews with 75 of the 80 persons known to have attended the church supper.

Epi Info 7 User Guide - Com Ref Sort command (https://www.cdc.gov/epiinfo/user-guide/command-reference/analysis-commands-sort.html). The SORT command syntax allows multiple field keys in sequence. The onset.date and onset.time fields exist in the Oswego dataset.

privileged_info_summary: The Oswego dataset is real historical epidemiological data from an actual gastrointestinal illness outbreak that occurred on April 18, 1940, in Oswego County, New York. The dataset contains 75 total records (persons interviewed), of which 46 reported illness. The outbreak was caused by contaminated vanilla ice cream served at a church supper. The dataset includes variables for: id, age, sex, meal time, illness status (coded as Yes/No), symptom onset date, symptom onset time, and various food consumption items. The expected columns (ID, AGE, SEX, ONSETDATE, ONSETTIME) are verified to exist in the actual dataset. The row count range (40-50) correctly encompasses the 46 ill cases. The total unfiltered rows (75) is accurate.
